items.title: Victoria ended logging. Now it's using another state's native forests

children.content: Four Corners follows the logs, and the money, to uncover how efforts to end logging have shifted the timber trade across state borders.

gallery.altText: A photo shot through the windscreen of a car, showing a truck loaded with logs ahead on a semi-suburban street.

thumbnail.altText: A photo shot through the windscreen of a car, showing a truck loaded with logs ahead on a semi-suburban street.

items.title: Iran briefing: Trump threatens Iran's negotiators as two sides meet

children.content: Donald Trump warns the Iranian delegation they might not return home from talks in Switzerland talks as the US and Iran argue over the fate of the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: Man with Jerusalem in the background

items.title: British PM set to resign after challenger's thumping by-election win

children.content: Less than two years after being elected in a landslide, the Labour PM is expected to announce his resignation to make way for another leader.

image.altText: Keir Starmer looking down slightly with a neutral expression, walking towards the camera.

items.title: There's more to housing demand than population growth

children.content: The problem for both the Coalition and Labor is that things are only going to get more difficult for ordinary families in the years ahead, and the economic environment is likely to keep favouring One Nation's message.

image.altText: A woman with orange hair and a blue suit gestures while speaking

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Black swan 'wipe-out' predicted as dead birds reported along WA coast

children.content: More than a dozen dead birds that washed up on southern WA beaches are reported to authorities amid concerns an outbreak of avian influenza is spreading.

image.altText: A black swan on a river.

images.altText: Nathan Ngoy is shown a red card

articles.title: How Australia's World Cup chef knows the players like his food

children.content: Chef Vini Capovilla has learnt a thing or two catering for the Socceroos at four World Cups. And one is finding out if they like his food.

image.altText: The Socceroos have a team barista in the hotel

articles.title: How is the most culturally diverse World Cup ever being received in Trump's America?

children.content: Fans from all over the world are being welcomed by locals across North America to celebrate the World Cup. But can it make a lasting difference in the US where President Donald Trump has cracked down on immigration?

image.altText: A group of Haiti football fans cheer inside the stadium

children.content: Lamine Yamal finds the net on his World Cup starting debut, as Spain makes light work of Saudi Arabia to bounce back from its shock draw with Cabo Verde. Follow live.

avatar.alt: A headshot of a man with sandy brown hair and clear plastic glasses smiling at the camera. Blue collared shirt.

items.title: Belgium down to 10 men after dominating Iran in stalemate
Concerns over AI and data centres a dangerous force for Labor

children.content: While the government is trying to assure people there is nothing to fear from AI, many think technology is moving too quickly for authorities to keep up.

image.altText: A man in a vampire outfit lies on the steps of an office building with protest signs against data centres behind him.

items.title: 'Compelling': Aussie used AI to design cancer treatment for his dog

children.content: Rosie the dog had her tumours treated by an mRNA vaccine developed with the help of artificial intelligence. Her owner had decided to explore treatment options using AI chatbots.

image.altText: Paul and Rosie

items.title: A major change to super payments will start next week

children.content: Many businesses support the payday superannuation changes, but some are concerned they will not be able to meet the obligations.

articles.title: Think writing off something on tax makes it free? It doesn't

children.content: Advertisers tout EOFY sales as a prime time for big buys, and hint at the promise of a generous tax refund, but that's not quite how it works.

image.altText: A collage of a series of end-of-financial-year sales tickets.

items.title: Fijians fear cartels will come looking for cocaine bricks washed up on beach

children.content: Twenty-seven bricks of cocaine have washed up around Fiji's remote Komo Island in recent weeks. Residents are worried about what the find will bring.

image.altText: a Fijian man on a beach looking out to sea

items.title: Auction clearance rates fall to lowest level in six years

children.content: Not since the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic have sellers seen such disappointing results at auction, with Cotality predicting "a further loss of momentum" in the housing market.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit gesticulates at an auction with a house behind

items.title: France on 'red alert' as temps hit 40C in Europe heatwave

children.content: Half of France is set to be placed on "red alert" as temperatures climb beyond 40 degrees Celsius across mainland Europe.

items.title: Betting apps know more about you than you might think

children.content: Betting ads on TV have been front of mind in Canberra lately, but experts say we're overlooking the real firepower behind Australia's gambling industry: cutting-edge, hyper-personalised, online tech.

image.altText: A photo over the shoulder of a woman as she looks at a gambling app on her phone.

items.title: Serena Williams to make Wimbledon singles comeback

children.content: Serena Williams will make a stunning return to singles at this year's Wimbledon after accepting the final wildcard berth by the All England Club.

image.altText: Serena Williams with thw 2016 Wimbledon singles trophy.

items.title: Dancing grandmothers, wastelands and rug making as we Race Around The World

children.content: From rug making in Morocco to small-town struggles in China, catch up on the films from the third week of the new season of Race Around The World.

items.title: Preserving lesbian history, one portrait at a time

children.content: When Stacey Bennett came out at 14, she felt alone. Now the Geelong-based artist has painted 100 lesbians to ensure future generations know they are part of a rich and diverse community.

image.altText: A woman with curly blonde hair sitting in front of a wall of portraits of women she has painted.

items.title: Question mark over duck hunt zone's future

children.content: Duck hunters and wildlife rescuers question whether Geelong's hunting zone will be axed due to the influx of new residents so close to shooting.

items.title: Delays to alcohol delivery restrictions worry SA anti-violence advocates

children.content: Family violence prevention advocates say the SA government has yet to progress alcohol delivery legislation as recommended by last year's Royal Commission into Domestic, Family and Sexual Violence.

items.title: Why fuel excise cut is being extended by another month but at smaller discount

items.title: Dry winter conditions forecast for Queensland before rain returns

children.content: After a week of unseasonal rainfall, much of the state will return to typical winter conditions from Tuesday, with drier, cooler mornings and sunny afternoons.

items.title: On health and child care, Labor might have found Pauline Hanson's kryptonite

children.content: Pauline Hanson said thousands of words in her marathon appearance at the National Press Club, but only a few of them will be heard widely.

children.content: Spencer Johnson proves to be a menace with the ball for Australia, as the tourists complete a T20I series clean sweep against Bangladesh.

items.title: The UK's version of One Nation just got smashed. Could our PM learn from it?

children.content: Britain's unpopular Labour government just scored a thumping by-election victory, but what could look like a blueprint for combating a populist opponent could actually be a warning for Anthony Albanese.

items.title: Opposition backs Israel's right to military action in Lebanon

children.content: The Shadow Foreign Affairs Minister has defended Israel’s right to continue its military operation in Lebanon, despite an MOU between the US and Iran which commits to an end to the war.

image.altText: Ted O’Brien at a press conference.

items.title: They bought into Australia's tallest tower. Now their deposits are in limbo

children.content: Apartment buyers who were sold on ambitious plans for the tallest building in the country say they're facing up to a decade of uncertainty after the head-turning project faltered.

image.altText: a mock up image of what the tower would look like when complete.

items.title: Bulldogs get the points at Docklands amid big injury worries for Saints

children.content: The Western Bulldogs get the points at Docklands against St Kilda to finish round 15, as the Saints lose two key players to injury.

image.altText: A Western Bulldogs tall forward is surrounded by teammates congratulating him after a vital goal.

items.title: Sharks antagonist raises Roosters' ire as Tricolours run through the finish

children.content: The Roosters finish with a wet sail to beat the Sharks 27-8 as Mark Nawaqanitawase gets a double in a heated but one-sided battle with opposite number Ronaldo Mulitalo.

image.altText: Roosters wrestle with ROnaldo Mulitalo during an NRL game.

items.title: Mona unveils $100m underground library packed with rare literary treasures

children.content: From Shakespeare's First Folio to neon-lit shelves to a curvy kids' section, Mona's new library, Phrontisterion,
is anything but ordinary.
